   113	def encode_prompt(task, template, train_samples, eval_sample, tokenizer, max_length, sfc=False, icl_sfc=False, generation=False, generation_with_gold=False, max_new_tokens=None):
   114	    """
   115	    Encode prompts for eval_sample
   116	    Input: 
   117	    - task, template: task and template class
   118	    - train_samples, eval_sample: demonstrations and the actual sample
   119	    - tokenizer, max_length: tokenizer and max length
   120	    - sfc: generate prompts for calibration (surface form competition; https://arxiv.org/abs/2104.08315)
   121	    - icl_sfc: generate prompts for ICL version calibration
   122	    - generation: whether it is an generation task
   123	    - generation_with_gold: whether to include the generation-task gold answers (for training)
   124	    - max_new_tokens: max number of new tokens to generate so that we can save enough space 
   125	      (only for generation tasks)
   126	    Output:
   127	    - encodings: a list of N lists of tokens. N is the number of options for classification/multiple-choice.
   128	    - option_lens: a list of N integers indicating the number of option tokens.
   129	    """
   130	
   131	    # Demonstrations for ICL
   132	    train_prompts = [template.verbalize(sample, sample.correct_candidate).strip() for sample in train_samples]
   133	    train_prompts = task.train_sep.join(train_prompts).strip()
   134	    
   135	    # sfc or icl_sfc indicates that this example is used for calibration
   136	    if sfc or icl_sfc:
   137	        encode_fn = template.encode_sfc; verbalize_fn = template.verbalize_sfc
   138	    else: 
   139	        encode_fn = template.encode; verbalize_fn = template.verbalize 
   140	            
   141	    unverbalized_eval_prompt = encode_fn(eval_sample).strip(' ')
   142	    if not generation:
   143	        # We generate one prompt for each candidate (different classes in classification)
   144	        # or different choices in multiple-choice tasks
   145	        verbalized_eval_prompts = [verbalize_fn(eval_sample, cand).strip(' ') for cand in eval_sample.candidates]
   146	        unverbalized_eval_prompt_length = len(tokenizer.encode(unverbalized_eval_prompt))
   147	        option_lens = [(len(tokenizer.encode(verbalized_eval_prompt)) - unverbalized_eval_prompt_length) for verbalized_eval_prompt in verbalized_eval_prompts]
   148	
   149	        if sfc:
   150	            # Without demonstrations
   151	            final_prompts = verbalized_eval_prompts 
   152	        else:
   153	            # With demonstrations
   154	            final_prompts = [(train_prompts + task.train_sep + eval_prompt).lstrip().strip(' ') for eval_prompt in verbalized_eval_prompts] 
   155	    else:
   156	        assert not sfc and not icl_sfc, "Generation tasks do not support SFC"
   157	        if generation_with_gold:
   158	            verbalized_eval_prompts = [verbalize_fn(eval_sample, eval_sample.correct_candidate)]
   159	            unverbalized_eval_prompt_length = len(tokenizer.encode(unverbalized_eval_prompt))
   160	            option_lens = [(len(tokenizer.encode(verbalized_eval_prompt)) - unverbalized_eval_prompt_length) for verbalized_eval_prompt in verbalized_eval_prompts]
   161	            final_prompts = [(train_prompts + task.train_sep + eval_prompt).lstrip().strip(' ') for eval_prompt in verbalized_eval_prompts] 
   162	        else:
   163	            option_lens = [0]
   164	            final_prompts = [(train_prompts + task.train_sep + unverbalized_eval_prompt).lstrip().strip(' ')]
   165	
   166	    # Tokenize 
   167	    encodings = [tokenizer.encode(final_prompt) for final_prompt in final_prompts]
   168	
   169	    # Truncate (left truncate as demonstrations are less important)
   170	    if generation and max_new_tokens is not None:
   171	        max_length = max_length - max_new_tokens
   172	
   173	    if any([len(encoding) > max_length for encoding in encodings]):
   174	        logger.warn("Exceed max length")
   175	    if tokenizer.add_bos_token:
   176	        encodings = [encoding[0:1] + encoding[1:][-(max_length-1):] for encoding in encodings]  
   177	    else:
   178	        encodings = [encoding[-max_length:] for encoding in encodings]  
   179	   
   180	    return encodings, option_lens

   184	@dataclass
   185	class ICLCollator:
   186	    """
   187	    Collator for ICL
   188	    """
   189	    tokenizer: PreTrainedTokenizerBase
   190	
   191	    def __call__(self, features: List[Dict[str, Any]]) -> Dict[str, Any]:
   192	        if not isinstance(features[0], Mapping):
   193	            features = [vars(f) for f in features]
   194	        first = features[0]
   195	        batch = {}
   196	        
   197	        pad_id = self.tokenizer.pad_token_id
   198	
   199	        pad_ids = {"input_ids": pad_id, "attention_mask": 0, "sfc_input_ids": pad_id, "sfc_attention_mask": 0, "labels": pad_id}
   200	        for key in first:
   201	            pp = pad_ids[key]
   202	            lens = [len(f[key]) for f in features]
   203	            max_len = max(lens)
   204	            feature = np.stack([np.pad(f[key], (0, max_len - lens[i]), "constant", constant_values=(0, pp)) for i, f in enumerate(features)])
   205	            padded_feature = torch.from_numpy(feature).long()
   206	            batch[key] = padded_feature
   207	            
   208	        return batch

   241	@dataclass
   242	class NondiffCollator(DataCollatorMixin):
   243	    """
   244	    Collator for non-differentiable objectives
   245	    """
   246	    tokenizer: PreTrainedTokenizerBase
   247	    padding: Union[bool, str, PaddingStrategy] = True
   248	    max_length: Optional[int] = None
   249	    pad_to_multiple_of: Optional[int] = None
   250	    label_pad_token_id: int = -100
   251	    return_tensors: str = "pt"
   252	
   253	    def torch_call(self, features):
   254	        import torch
   255	
   256	        label_name = "label" if "label" in features[0].keys() else "labels"
   257	        labels = [feature[label_name] for feature in features] if label_name in features[0].keys() else None
   258	
   259	        no_labels_features = [{k: v for k, v in feature.items() if k != label_name and k != "gold"} for feature in features]
   260	
   261	        batch = self.tokenizer.pad(
   262	            no_labels_features,
   263	            padding=self.padding,
   264	            max_length=self.max_length,
   265	            pad_to_multiple_of=self.pad_to_multiple_of,
   266	            return_tensors="pt",
   267	        )
   268	
   269	        if labels is None:
   270	            return batch
   271	
   272	        sequence_length = batch["input_ids"].shape[1]
   273	        padding_side = self.tokenizer.padding_side
   274	
   275	        def to_list(tensor_or_iterable):
   276	            if isinstance(tensor_or_iterable, torch.Tensor):
   277	                return tensor_or_iterable.tolist()
   278	            return list(tensor_or_iterable)
   279	
   280	        if padding_side == "right":
   281	            batch[label_name] = [
   282	                to_list(label) + [self.label_pad_token_id] * (sequence_length - len(label)) for label in labels
   283	            ]
   284	        else:
   285	            batch[label_name] = [
   286	                [self.label_pad_token_id] * (sequence_length - len(label)) + to_list(label) for label in labels
   287	            ]
   288	
   289	        batch[label_name] = torch.tensor(batch[label_name], dtype=torch.int64)
   290	        if "gold" in features[0]:
   291	            batch["gold"] = [feature["gold"] for feature in features]
   292	        
   293	        return batch

   296	class SIGUSR1Callback(transformers.TrainerCallback):
   297	    """
   298	    This callback is used to save the model when a SIGUSR1 signal is received
   299	    (SLURM stop signal or a keyboard interruption signal).
   300	    """
   301	
   302	    def __init__(self) -> None:
   303	        super().__init__()
   304	        self.signal_received = False
   305	        signal.signal(signal.SIGUSR1, self.handle_signal)
   306	        signal.signal(signal.SIGINT, self.handle_signal)
   307	        logger.warn("Handler registered")
   308	
   309	    def handle_signal(self, signum, frame):
   310	        self.signal_received = True
   311	        logger.warn("Signal received")
   312	
   313	    def on_step_end(self, args, state, control, **kwargs):
   314	        if self.signal_received:
   315	            control.should_save = True
   316	            control.should_training_stop = True
   317	
   318	    def on_train_end(self, args, state, control, **kwargs):
   319	        if self.signal_received:
   320	            exit(0)
